notes: Live product read capture now includes both the merchant-facing detail slice for a real product with empty collections/media connections and a dedicated empty-state fixture covering missing-product null behavior plus empty `products` / `productsCount` results for an unmatched query.

Live product search-grammar capture exercises quoted phrases, bare text terms, and term-local negation across both `products` and `productsCount` without conflating that slice with the broader grouped/prefix advanced-search fixture.

Live product advanced search capture exercises trailing prefix matching, grouped OR vendor filters, grouped negation, and UPDATED_AT-desc ordering with pageInfo across both `products` and `productsCount`.

Live product search pagination capture preserves a concrete filtered UPDATED_AT-desc window plus forward and backward cursor traversal so overlay pagination parity can be compared against Shopify for the same query tuple.

Live product OR-precedence capture records Shopify's current AND-before-OR behavior for unparenthesized search terms, preserving the merchant-facing products/productsCount slice plus UPDATED_AT-desc ordering and pagination metadata.

Live product sort-key capture now covers deterministic Shopify ordering for the current schema-backed product sort keys TITLE, VENDOR, PRODUCT_TYPE, and ID on the merchant-facing catalog slice used by the overlay serializer.

Live product relevance capture preserves Shopify opaque cursors plus RELEVANCE ordering for a concrete merchant-facing query so staged overlay replay can reuse the hydrated search baseline instead of inventing synthetic order.

The current variant/inventory-item read capture now includes the first inventory-level connection slice on `inventoryItem.inventoryLevels(first:)`, preserving location-aware level nodes plus `available` / `on_hand` / `incoming` quantity rows from live Shopify fixtures.
Live collection detail capture now covers top-level `collection(id:)` plus the merchant-relevant nested `products` connection slice used by the overlay serializer.
Live customer detail capture now covers top-level `customer(id:)` reads with the merchant-facing field slice already wired through the local overlay serializer.
Live customers catalog capture now covers top-level `customers` pagination with the merchant-facing customer card slice used by the current overlay serializer.
Live customer search capture now covers top-level `customers(query:, sortKey:, reverse:)` replay for a concrete `state:DISABLED` slice ordered by `UPDATED_AT` descending.

Live customer advanced search capture now covers prefix matching, grouped OR tag filters, and grouped negation for top-level `customers(query:, sortKey:, reverse:)` replay ordered by `UPDATED_AT` descending.
Live customer sort-key capture now covers the deterministic Shopify customer sort keys `NAME`, `ID`, and `LOCATION` against the merchant-facing customer card slice used by the overlay serializer.

Live customer relevance capture now covers `customers(query:, sortKey: RELEVANCE)` with a concrete broad-text query so the proxy can replay Shopify's opaque relevance ordering, cursors, and pageInfo without inventing deterministic score semantics.

Live customersCount capture now covers top-level `customersCount(query:)`; on this host the captured `email:` and `state:` query slices both returned exact total counts plus Shopify `invalid_field` warnings under `extensions.search[]`, so the proxy keeps a conservative no-op count-query model and now mirrors that warning metadata instead of assuming `customers` list-search parity.
Live safe-write capture now covers `customerCreate` plus immediate downstream `customer`, `customers`, and `customersCount` visibility for the supported customer card/detail slice.
Live safe-write capture now covers `customerUpdate` plus immediate downstream `customer` and filtered `customers` replay for the supported customer card/detail slice.
Live safe-write capture now covers `customerDelete` plus immediate downstream customer removal from `customer`, `customers`, and `customersCount` reads.
Live safe-write capture now covers collectionAddProducts plus immediate downstream `collection.products` / `product.collections` membership parity.
Live safe-write capture now covers collectionCreate plus immediate downstream `collection` / `collections` read parity.
Live safe-write capture now covers collectionDelete plus immediate downstream `collection` / `collections` removal parity.
Live safe-write capture now covers collectionRemoveProducts plus immediate downstream `collection.products` / `product.collections` membership parity.
Live safe-write capture now covers collectionUpdate plus immediate downstream `collection` / nested `product.collections` parity.
Live collections catalog capture now covers top-level `collections` pagination together with a nested merchant-facing `products` slice.
Live locations catalog capture now covers top-level `locations` pagination for the merchant-facing location slice (`id`, `name`) that the inventory overlay can derive from effective inventory levels.

Live product-publication capture now also records the top-level `publications` catalog slice (`id`, `name`) so the proxy can replay publication targets locally when staged product publication state exists.

Live inventory item update capture now covers the current merchant-facing metadata slice (`tracked`, `requiresShipping`, origin codes, harmonized system code, and `measurement.weight`) together with immediate downstream `inventoryItem` visibility and the unknown-id `does not exist` userError path.

Live inventory adjustment capture now covers `inventoryAdjustQuantities`. Shopify mirrored each `available` delta into an additional `on_hand` change, left `quantityAfterChange` null in the mutation payload, updated `productVariant` / `inventoryItem.variant` quantities before product-level `totalInventory` and `inventory_total:` catalog search caught up, accepted non-available names like `incoming` only when each change carried a ledger document URI, and rejected invalid names like `on_hand` with an explicit valid-values error plus the same ledger document URI requirement.

Live inventory linkage capture now covers the first safe `inventoryActivate` slice on this host: calling the root against the already-active only location is a no-op without `available`, while providing `available` returns the captured active-location userError.
Live inventory linkage capture now covers the current single-location `inventoryDeactivate` error path on this host, including the exact minimum-one-location userError with `field: null`.

Live inventory linkage capture now covers the first safe `inventoryBulkToggleActivation` slice on this host: `activate: true` against the already-active only location is a no-op success, while `activate: false` returns the captured `CANNOT_DEACTIVATE_FROM_ONLY_LOCATION` userError.

Live current-root capture for `metafieldsDelete` now closes the singular `metafieldDelete` path as a compatibility alias. The compatibility shim still returns `deletedId`, but it stages the same owner-scoped downstream metafield removal proven against Shopify's plural root.
Live safe-write capture now covers `metafieldsSet` together with immediate downstream `product.metafield(...)` / `product.metafields` visibility for product-owned metafields.

Live safe-write capture now covers Shopify's current plural `metafieldsDelete` root together with immediate downstream `product.metafield(...)` / `product.metafields` removal parity for product-owned metafields.

Live capture recorded for `productChangeStatus`, and the staged runtime now keeps the mutation payload and immediate downstream status-filter reads aligned closely enough to promote the operation as covered.

Live safe-write capture now covers productCreateMedia plus immediate downstream `product.media` parity. Shopify returns newly created image media as `UPLOADED` with null image urls in the mutation payload, then flips the immediate follow-up read into a null-url `PROCESSING` state before the asset becomes `READY`.

Live safe-write capture now covers productDeleteMedia plus immediate downstream `product.media` removal parity, including Shopify returning an empty `deletedProductImageIds` list for MediaImage deletes.

Live safe-write capture now covers `productDuplicate` against a rich source product graph. On this host, Shopify preserved collection memberships and product metafields on the duplicate, but the duplicate's immediate `media` connection came back empty even when the source product already had ready image media.

Live safe-write capture now covers productOptionUpdate plus immediate downstream `product.options` parity, including Shopify's current behavior where renaming/repositioning the sole option still serializes it back at position `1` and only variant-backed values appear in `values`.

Live safe-write capture now covers productOptionsCreate plus immediate downstream `product.options` parity, including Shopify replacing the default Title-only state with the created option and marking the first created value as variant-backed immediately.

Live safe-write capture now covers productOptionsDelete plus immediate downstream `product.options` parity, including Shopify restoring a fresh Title/Default Title fallback option after deleting the only custom option.

Minimal live publish/unpublish mutation payloads now capture successfully for `productPublish`, and this scenario now tracks the ready-for-comparison minimal live payload slice separately from the still-blocked aggregate publication-field companion scenario.

The aggregate publication-field blocker for `productPublish` remains explicit in this separate captured scenario: selecting `publishedOnCurrentPublication`, `availablePublicationsCount`, or `resourcePublicationsCount` still fails even though the minimal mutation payload now captures successfully.

Live safe-write capture now covers the synchronous create slice of `productSet` plus immediate downstream `product(id:)` parity. The current live API requires `variants[].inventoryQuantities[]` entries to include both `locationId` and quantity `name` (for this capture, `available`) rather than only a bare quantity.

Minimal live publish/unpublish mutation payloads now capture successfully for `productUnpublish`, and this scenario now tracks the ready-for-comparison minimal live payload slice separately from the still-blocked aggregate publication-field companion scenario.

The aggregate publication-field blocker for `productUnpublish` remains explicit in this separate captured scenario: selecting `publishedOnCurrentPublication`, `availablePublicationsCount`, or `resourcePublicationsCount` still fails even though the minimal mutation payload now captures successfully.
Live safe-write capture now covers productUpdateMedia once the uploaded asset becomes `READY`; attempting the same update earlier returns `mediaUserErrors` instead of a successful mutation payload.

Compatibility-only coverage for `productVariantCreate`: the local wrapper rides the same normalized variant overlay proven by captured `productVariantsBulkCreate` live parity, and the checked-in schema blocker note records that the direct legacy root is absent on the current 2025-01 conformance store.

Compatibility-only coverage for `productVariantDelete`: the local wrapper reuses the bulk delete overlay path already settled against live Shopify, and the checked-in schema blocker note records that the direct legacy root is absent on the current 2025-01 conformance store.

Compatibility-only coverage for `productVariantUpdate`: the local wrapper reuses the bulk update overlay path already settled against live Shopify, and the checked-in schema blocker note records that the direct legacy root is absent on the current 2025-01 conformance store.

Live safe-write capture now covers `productVariantsBulkCreate` on the current token without location-scoped inventory quantities. Shopify accepted `optionValues` plus `inventoryItem.sku`, returned the created variant immediately on `product.variants`, but left downstream `products(query: "sku:...")` / `productsCount(query: "sku:...")` empty immediately after the write.

Live safe-write capture now covers `productVariantsBulkDelete`, including immediate downstream `product.variants` removal parity while sku-filtered `products` / `productsCount` still remain empty on the same just-written product surface.

Live safe-write capture now covers `productVariantsBulkUpdate`. Shopify accepted `inventoryItem.sku` on update, kept `inventoryQuantity` at `0` without a separate inventory adjustment, and left downstream sku-filtered `products` / `productsCount` empty immediately after the write.

Live capture recorded for `tagsAdd`, and the staged runtime now mirrors Shopify's immediate tag-index lag for hydrated products: `product.tags` updates immediately while `products(query: "tag:...")` / `productsCount(query: "tag:...")` stay limited to hydrated/base tags.

Live capture recorded for `tagsRemove`, and the staged runtime now mirrors Shopify's immediate tag-index lag for hydrated products by dropping removed hydrated/base tags from search immediately while leaving newly added-only tags out of `products(query: "tag:...")` / `productsCount(query: "tag:...")` until rehydration.
Live safe-write capture now covers productCreate plus immediate downstream product read parity.
Live safe-write capture now covers productUpdate plus immediate downstream product read parity.
Live safe-write capture now covers productDelete plus immediate downstream null/list-removal parity.

Live direct-order read probing on this host is currently safe enough to capture a conservative empty-state baseline: `order(id: gid://shopify/Order/0) -> null`, `orders(first: 1, sortKey: CREATED_AT, reverse: true) -> empty connection`, and `ordersCount -> { count: 0, precision: EXACT }`.

A first live-backed draft-order detail slice is now captured: reading `draftOrder(id:)` immediately after a safe `draftOrderCreate` returns the same merchant-facing open-draft detail payload on this host.

A first non-empty live `draftOrders(first: ...)` catalog baseline is now captured on this host: the current repo credential can read the newest-first draft-order catalog with opaque Shopify cursors and a non-empty page window immediately after safe draft-order creation.

A first non-empty live `draftOrdersCount(query:)` baseline is now captured on this host: the current repo credential can read the aggregate draft-order count/precision slice for the same catalog family without relying only on staged synthetic counts.

A first evidence-backed filtered draft-order search slice is now captured on this host: `draftOrders(query: "email:...")` and `draftOrdersCount(query: "email:...")` do not narrow the result set and instead return Shopify `extensions.search[]` invalid_field warnings for the unsupported email field.

A third safe live order-editing slice is now captured for `orderUpdate`: omitting inline `input.id` fails earlier with top-level GraphQL `missingRequiredInputObjectAttribute` instead of reaching mutation-scoped `userErrors`.

A fourth safe live order-editing slice is now captured for `orderUpdate`: inline `input.id: null` fails earlier with top-level GraphQL `argumentLiteralsIncompatible` instead of reaching mutation-scoped `userErrors`.

A second safe live order-editing slice is now captured for `orderUpdate`: omitting `input.id` from the variables payload fails earlier with a top-level GraphQL `INVALID_VARIABLE` error (`Expected value to not be null`) instead of reaching mutation-scoped `userErrors`.

A safe live order-editing slice is now captured for `orderUpdate`: updating a missing order id returns `order: null` plus `userErrors[{ field: ['id'], message: 'Order does not exist' }]` under the current host credential without requiring a writable order target.

The first live happy-path `orderUpdate` slice is now captured on this host: updating `note` and `tags` on a freshly created order returned `userErrors: []`, kept the same order id/name, and the immediate downstream `order(id:)` read preserved the edited values.

The first fulfillment-domain slice is a safe invalid-id validation branch: on this host `fulfillmentCreate` against `gid://shopify/FulfillmentOrder/0` returns top-level GraphQL `RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND` / `invalid id` instead of a scope blocker.

A first safe fulfillmentTrackingInfoUpdate validation slice is now captured for the orders domain: omitting the inline required `fulfillmentId` argument fails with top-level GraphQL `missingRequiredArguments` before Shopify reaches the root's separate fulfillment-write scope and permission gate.

A second safe fulfillmentTrackingInfoUpdate validation slice is now captured for the orders domain: inline `fulfillmentId: null` fails with top-level GraphQL `argumentLiteralsIncompatible` before Shopify reaches the root's separate fulfillment-write scope and permission gate.

A safe fulfillmentTrackingInfoUpdate validation slice is now captured for the orders domain: omitting the required `$fulfillmentId` variable fails at GraphQL coercion time with `INVALID_VARIABLE` before Shopify reaches the root's separate fulfillment-write scope and permission gate.

A first safe fulfillmentCancel validation slice is now captured for the orders domain: omitting the inline required `id` argument fails with top-level GraphQL `missingRequiredArguments` before Shopify reaches the root's separate fulfillment lifecycle access gate.

A second safe fulfillmentCancel validation slice is now captured for the orders domain: inline `id: null` fails with top-level GraphQL `argumentLiteralsIncompatible` before Shopify reaches the root's separate fulfillment lifecycle access gate.

A safe fulfillmentCancel validation slice is now captured for the orders domain: omitting the required `$id` variable fails at GraphQL coercion time with `INVALID_VARIABLE` before Shopify reaches the root's separate fulfillment lifecycle access gate.

First live fulfillmentTrackingInfoUpdate parity scaffold for the orders domain. The current conformance credential on this host is blocked on one of Shopify's fulfillment-write scopes plus the fulfill_and_ship_orders permission, so the scenario stays planned-with-blocker until a fulfillment-lifecycle-capable install is available.

First live fulfillmentCancel parity scaffold for the orders domain. The current conformance credential on this host still returns ACCESS_DENIED for this mutation, so the scenario stays planned-with-blocker until a fulfillment-lifecycle-capable install is available.

A safe direct-order creation validation slice is now captured for `orderCreate`: omitting the inline required `order` argument fails with top-level GraphQL `missingRequiredArguments` before Shopify reaches the root's separate offline-token/write_orders blocker.

A second safe direct-order creation validation slice is now captured for `orderCreate`: inline `order: null` fails with top-level GraphQL `argumentLiteralsIncompatible` before Shopify reaches the root's separate offline-token/write_orders blocker.

A first safe order-creation validation slice is now captured for `orderCreate`: omitting the required `$order` variable fails at GraphQL coercion time with `INVALID_VARIABLE` before Shopify reaches the root's separate offline-token/write_orders blocker.

The first merchant-realistic direct-order creation slice is now live-captured on this host: `orderCreate` accepted custom line items, shipping lines, billing + shipping addresses, and order customAttributes, returned a paid unfulfilled order payload, and the created order was immediately readable through `order(id:)`.

A second safe draft-order creation validation slice is now captured for `draftOrderCreate`: omitting the inline required `input` argument fails with top-level GraphQL `missingRequiredArguments` before Shopify reaches the root's separate draft-order write/manage-access blocker.

A third safe draft-order creation validation slice is now captured for `draftOrderCreate`: inline `input: null` fails with top-level GraphQL `argumentLiteralsIncompatible` before Shopify reaches the root's separate draft-order write/manage-access blocker.

A first safe draft-order creation validation slice is now captured for `draftOrderCreate`: omitting the required `$input` variable fails at GraphQL coercion time with `INVALID_VARIABLE` before Shopify reaches the root's separate draft-order write/manage-access blocker.

The first merchant-realistic draft-order creation slice is now live-captured on this host: `draftOrderCreate` accepted custom line items, addresses, tags, and customAttributes, returned an immediate open-draft payload, and the created draft was then readable through `draftOrder(id:)`.

A second safe draft-order completion validation slice is now captured for `draftOrderComplete`: omitting the inline required `id` argument fails with top-level GraphQL `missingRequiredArguments` before Shopify reaches the root's separate draft-order completion access/permission blocker.

A third safe draft-order completion validation slice is now captured for `draftOrderComplete`: inline `id: null` fails with top-level GraphQL `argumentLiteralsIncompatible` before Shopify reaches the root's separate draft-order completion access/permission blocker.

A first safe draft-order completion validation slice is now captured for `draftOrderComplete`: omitting the required `$id` variable fails at GraphQL coercion time with `INVALID_VARIABLE` before Shopify reaches the root's separate draft-order completion access/permission blocker.

First live draftOrderComplete parity scaffold for the orders domain. The current conformance credential on this host is blocked on write_draft_orders plus mark-as-paid/set-payment-terms permission, so the scenario stays planned-with-blocker until a draft-order completion-capable install is available.

A first safe order-editing validation slice is now captured for `orderEditBegin`: omitting the required `$id` variable fails at GraphQL coercion time with `INVALID_VARIABLE` before Shopify reaches the root's separate `write_order_edits` blocker.

First live orderEditBegin parity scaffold for the orders domain. The current conformance credential on this host is blocked on write_order_edits, so the scenario stays planned-with-blocker until an order-edit capable install is available.

A second safe order-editing validation slice is now captured for `orderEditAddVariant`: omitting the required `$id` variable fails at GraphQL coercion time with `INVALID_VARIABLE` before Shopify reaches the root's separate `write_order_edits` blocker.

First live orderEditAddVariant parity scaffold for the orders domain. The current conformance credential on this host is blocked on write_order_edits, so the scenario stays planned-with-blocker until an order-edit capable install is available.

First live orderEditCommit parity scaffold for the orders domain. The current conformance credential on this host is blocked on write_order_edits, so the scenario stays planned-with-blocker until an order-edit capable install is available.
